extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates cases against the Company, Subsidiaries, Directors other than Promoters, Promoters, KMP and SMP from cases by those parties. I counted only against rows: criminal proceedings are 1 against a Director other than a Promoter, statutory/regulatory actions are Nil across the against rows, and tax proceedings against the Company are 3; tax matters against Directors and Promoters were excluded from the company-and-subsidiaries tax count. The quantified aggregate against the Company and Subsidiaries is the Company row total of ₹3.50 million, converted to ₹0.35 crore; subsidiary quantified matters are Nil, while promoter/director tax amounts of ₹0.10 million and ₹9.82 million were excluded. The contingent-liabilities figure is the explicitly stated total of ₹3,034.32 million as at June 30, 2025, converted to ₹303.432 crore, rather than a re-sum of components. Company-wide customer concentration for the most recent full fiscal year, Fiscal 2025, is top 5 at 66.12% and top 10 at 76.58%, based on revenue from operations/total revenue as presented in the customer table; the separate Pearson, industry-vertical and geography percentages were not used because they are not top-customer company-wide figures. The litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million: ₹1 million = ₹0.1 crore, so ₹3.50 million = ₹0.35 crore and ₹3,034.32 million = ₹303.432 crore.
